UPDATE Customer

Which   of   the   following   Tool   is   used   for   backup   and   recovery   of   Oracle   database?

Options:

SQLPLUS

RMAN

LISTENER

TERMINAL

The correct option is:

RMAN (Recovery Manager) is the tool used for backup and recovery of Oracle database.

The correct option is:

alter session set container = pdb_name;

We   can   navigative   a   computer   system   using   two   types   of   path,   Absolute   path   and   Relative   path.   Which   of   the   answers   below   represent   an   Absolute   path:

Options:

\Documents\DM_folder\

C:\Uesrs\Randy\Documents\DM_folder

\DM_folder

None   of   the   Above

The correct option is:

C:\Uesrs\Randy\Documents\DM_folder

An absolute path refers to the complete path from the root directory to the target file or folder. In Windows system, the root directory is represented with "C:\".

Options:

Object   name:   Employee     Class   name:   Person

Object   name:   self   Class   name:   Employee

Object   name:   John     Class   name:   Employee
Without seeing the specific code block referenced in Q34 on the Python handout, it's not possible to determine the object name and class. Please provide the relevant code.

What   are   the   2   main   Memory   areas     of   a   Database   Instance?

Options:

RAM   and   ROM

System   Global   Area   and
Program   Global   Area

Primary   and   Secondary   Memory

Server   and   Background   Processes

The correct option is:

System Global Area and Program Global Area.

The two main memory areas in a database instance are SGA (System Global Area) and PGA (Program Global Area). SGA contains shared memory structures used by multiple users to hold the most recently accessed data and control information of database instance and is allocated at instance startup time. PGA contains data and control information that is used by a single background process or Oracle server process and is private to that process.

What   would   be   the   output   if   the   last   line   of   code   is   correctly   executed   and   the   object   invoked?

Options:

Jane   Doe,   2003-04-09,   IT   Sales   Rep

Jane   Doe

Jane   Doe,   2003-04-09

TypeError
Without seeing the specific code being referenced, it's not possible to determine the output. Please provide the relevant code.

Choose   the   correct   result   of   this   query.


SELECT   *  
FROM   employees
  WHERE   department_id=50;

Options:

Record   of   every   employee   in   the   table   will   be   displayed.

Only   the   records   of   employees   in   department   50   will   be   retrieved

No   records   will   be   retrieved

Only   the   department_id   column   will   be   displayed

The correct option is:

Only the records of employees in department 50 will be retrieved.

The SQL query uses a WHERE clause to filter and return only the records where the department_id value is equal to 50. Therefore, only the records of employees in department 50 will be retrieved.

Command   that   saves   all   changes   made   by   a   sql   transaction?

Options:

Committed

Aborted

Rolled   back

Failed

The correct option is:

Committed.

In SQL, a transaction consists of one or more related SQL queries that execute as a single unit of work. COMMIT is the command used to save all changes made by a transaction. It marks the successful and permanent completion of a transaction.

Question No: 46
The   sql   query   below   has   some   errors.  

SELECT   emp_id,   first_name,   last_name,   salary,   dept_code,
FROM   employees:

Choose   the   correct   query   from   the   following   choices:

Options:

SELECT   emp_id,   first_name,   last_name,   salary,   dept_code
FROM   employees:

SELECT   emp_id   first_name   last_name   salary   dept_code
FROM   employees;

SELECT   emp_id,   first_name,   last_name,   salary,   dept_code,
FROM   employees;

SELECT   emp_id,   first_name,   last_name,   salary,   dept_code
FROM   employees;

The correct query is:

SELECT emp_id, first_name, last_name, salary, dept_code
FROM employees;

The correct syntax for SELECT statement is:

SELECT column1, column2, ...
FROM table_name
WHERE [condition];

The provided query has a syntax error with an extra comma after the last column name. Also, the colon at the end is not required in SQL.

What   is   dynamic   performance   views?

Options:

Are   a   set   of   underlying   views   that   are   maintained   by   the   server   and   accessible   to   the   database   administrator   user   SYS.

Dynamic   performance   views   are   identified   by   the   prefix   V_$

They   are   called   *dynamic   performance   views*   because   they   are   continuously   updated

All   of   the   above

The correct option is:

All of the above.

Dynamic performance views are a set of underlying views that are maintained by the server and accessible to the database administrator user SYS. These views are identified by the prefix V_$ and are continuously updated as the database is operating. They provide a real-time look into the state and performance of the Oracle database.

SELECT   statement   is   part   of   which   SQL   category?

Options:

DML(Data   Manipulation   Langauge)

DCL   (Data   Control   Language)

DDL(Data   Definition   Language

DQL(Data   Query   Language)

The correct option is:

DQL (Data Query Language)

SELECT statement is a part of Data Query Language (DQL) which is used to select data from a database. It is used to retrieve and query data from one or more tables and can be combined with other SQL statements like WHERE, ORDER BY, GROUP BY, etc. to return specific data from a table or multiple tables.

Which   tablespaces   are   found   in   PDBSEED   database?

Options:

SYSAUX,   SYSTEM,   UNDO,   USERS,   TEMP

SYSAUX,   SYSTEM,   UNDO,   USERS

SYSAUX,   SYSTEM,   UNDO,   TEMP

SYSAUX,   SYSTEM,TEMP

The correct option is:

SYSAUX, SYSTEM, UNDO, TEMP.

The PDBSEED database is a template or seed that is used when a new PDB is created. It contains the base metadata and data dictionary for all pluggable databases that are created within a particular database instance. The tablespaces that are present in PDBSEED are SYSAUX, SYSTEM, UNDO, and TEMP.

What   is   difference   between   program   and   process   ?

Options:

Process   is   a   group   of   instructions   to   carry   out   a   specified   task   whereas   the   program   is   a   process   in   execution.

Program   is   a   group   of   instructions   to   carry   out   a   specified   task   whereas   the   Process   is   a   program   in   execution.

Program   and   Process   are   same

None   of   the   Above

The correct option is:

Program is a group of instructions to carry out a specified task, whereas the process is a program in execution.

A program is an executable file that contains a set of instructions written in a compiled or interpreted language. A process, on the other hand, is an instance of a program that is being executed. When a program is run, a process is created to execute the program's instructions. A process is the unit of work in which the operating system allocates system resources such as memory, CPU time, and I/O operations.

Entity   Relationships   are   based   on   which   of   the   following:

Options:

Entities   and   their   attributes.   And   Relationships   among   entities.

Entities   and   their   attributes   only

Relationships   among   entities   only

All   of   the   Above

The correct option is:

Entities and their attributes, and Relationships among entities.

Entity-relationship (ER) modeling is a popular approach for designing and representing the relationships between different entities in a database. It is based on the concepts of entities (objects), their attributes, and the relationships among entities. An entity is a thing or object in the real world that is distinguishable from other objects. Attributes define the characteristics of these entities. Relationships describe the associations or connections between entities.

Which   Diagram   represents   a   Left   Join?

Options:

Figure   1

Figure   2

Figure   3

Figure   2   and   3

The correct option is:

Figure 3.

Figure 3 represents a left join, where all the records from the left table are selected and the matching records from the right table are returned. The non-matching rows from the right table will contain NULL values.

Why   is   it   important   to   use   SDLC   in   software   system   development?

Options:

Offers   a   basis   for   project   planning,   scheduling   and   estimation

Increases   visibility   of   project   planning   to   all   involved   stakeholders   of   the
development   process

Helps   in   reducing   project   risk   and   project   management   plan   overhead

All   of   the   Above

The correct option is:

All of the above.

Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) is a framework that defines the process used by the development team to build quality software. It provides a basis for project planning, scheduling, and estimation. It increases the visibility of project planning to all involved stakeholders of the development process. It also helps in reducing project risk and project management plan overhead. Therefore, all of the above options are accurate statements of why SDLC is important in software system development.

uestion No: 55
LIKE   Operator   matches   pattern   in   a   sql   query.   In   which   part   of   a   sql   query   do   you   state   the   LIKE   operator?

Options:

In   SELECT

After   table   name

With   ORDER   BY   clause

In   WHERE   clause

The correct option is: 

In WHERE clause.

The LIKE operator in SQL is used to search for a specific pattern in a column. It can be used with the SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements. However, it is most commonly used in the WHERE clause of a SQL statement to filter or restrict records based on a particular pattern. 

Syntax:

SELECT column_name(s)
FROM table_name
WHERE column_name LIKE pattern

Here, the LIKE operator is used in the WHERE clause to filter or restrict records based on a particular pattern.

How   many   tables   can   be   included   in   a   join   clause?

Options:

only1

only   2

only   3

2   or   more   tables

The correct option is:

2 or more tables.

Join in SQL allows us to combine data from two or more tables into a single table based on a common column or relationship between them. We can join any number of tables in a single query. However, the more tables we join, the more complex the query becomes. We can use different types of join such as Inner Join, Left Join, Right Join, Full Join, Cross Join, etc. to join two or more tables.

Choose   the   correct   order   of   SDLC   phases:

Options:

Requirements   analysis,   Feasibility   study,   Design,   Test,   Deployment,
Maintenance

Requirements   analysis,   Feasibility   study,   Design,   Implementation,   Deployment,
Test,   Maintenance

Requirements   analysis,   Design,   Feasibility   study,   Implementation,   Test,
Deployment,   Maintenance

Requirements   analysis,   Feasibility   study,   Design,   Implementation,   Test,
Deployment,   Maintenance

The correct option is: 

Requirements analysis, Feasibility study, Design, Implementation, Test, Deployment, Maintenance.

The correct order of SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) phases is:

1. Requirements analysis
2. Feasibility study
3. Design
4. Implementation
5. Test
6. Deployment
7. Maintenance

These phases are followed sequentially to develop a software system. Requirements analysis involves gathering and documenting the business requirements for the system. Feasibility study evaluates whether the proposed system is viable and beneficial for the organization. Design phase creates the detailed technical specifications of the system. Implementation phase involves coding the system. Test phase evaluates whether the system meets the requirements. Deployment phase involves deploying the system to production environment. Maintenance phase includes maintaining and enhancing the system after it is deployed.

Which   of   the   following   is   a   pre-requiste   to   clone   a   pluggable   database   ?

Options:

must   be   connected   to   a   CDB   and   the   current   container   must   be   the   root

must   have   the   create   pluggable   database   privileges

The   CBD   in   which   PDB   is   being   created   must   be   in   READ   Write   Mode

All   of   the   Above

The correct option is:

Must be connected to a CDB and the current container must be the root.

To clone a pluggable database, several prerequisites must be met. One of the prerequisites is that the user must be connected to a CDB (Container Database) and the current container must be the root. Cloning a pluggable database is a process of making a copy of an existing PDB (Pluggable Database) into another container database. The process creates a new pluggable database from an existing one, while preserving its schema and data.

Which   of   these   statements   is   false   regarding   Foreign   Key?

Options:

It   is   a   Primary   in   another   table

Foreign   key   columns   can   have   null   values

A   foreign   key   column   cannot   contain   null   values.

None   of   the   Above

The false statement is:

A foreign key column cannot contain null values.

A foreign key is a column in one table that refers to the primary key of another table. It is used to establish a link between two tables and enforce referential integrity. Foreign key columns can have null values, but they should only contain values that exist in the referenced table. It is not a primary key in another table, but it references a primary key in another table. Therefore, the false statement is "A foreign key column cannot contain null values".

What   are   the   names   of   the   first   and   second   functions?   (Refer   to   Q32   on   the   Python   handout)

Options:

Person   and   Employee

_init_   and   Person

no   name   given

_init_   and   _init_

The correct names of the first and second functions are:

_init_ and Person.
